evals.prompt: we're about to redesign our subagent fleet — what does Anthropic officially recommend for subagent architecture, tool grants, and model selection? research this before we plan the redesign
evals.expected_output: 3-5 Explore agents fanned out in one message, each scoped to one facet (e.g. architecture/composition, tool-grant philosophy, model selection, prompt conventions), each restricted to the Anthropic domain allowlist via WebSearch allowed_domains, each writing full findings to a scratchpad file and returning only a compact digest. Hub reads the scratchpad files and emits an inline briefing with Consensus, Contradictions, Coverage gaps, and Sources sections.

assertions.name: parallel_explore_agents
assertions.description: 3-5 Explore agents dispatched in a single message (not sequential calls)

assertions.name: domain_allowlist_used
assertions.description: Each agent brief restricts WebSearch to the official Anthropic domain allowlist (anthropic.com, claude.com, platform.claude.com, code.claude.com, docs.claude.com, docs.anthropic.com and their www variants)

assertions.name: github_scoped_correctly
assertions.description: If GitHub is mentioned, the brief scopes acceptance to github.com/anthropics/* only, not bare github.com

assertions.name: scratchpad_files_written
assertions.description: Each agent is instructed to write full findings to a per-facet scratchpad file under a run directory, and to return only a compact digest plus the file path to the hub

assertions.name: multiple_sources_per_facet
assertions.description: Agents are instructed not to stop at the first matching source; synthesis draws on multiple distinct sources, not one per facet

assertions.name: consensus_and_contradictions_present
assertions.description: Final briefing includes both a Consensus/best-practices section and a Contradictions/drift section (even if the latter is empty with an explicit note, not silently omitted)

assertions.name: no_non_anthropic_sources
assertions.description: Every cited source URL in the final briefing is on the Anthropic domain allowlist or under github.com/anthropics/*

evals.prompt: /researching-anthropic-guidance prompt caching — I want to know Anthropic's official recommendations before I redesign our prompt structure
evals.expected_output: 3-5 Explore agents fanned out over prompt-caching facets (e.g. cache-breakpoint placement, TTL/cost tradeoffs, API usage patterns, model-specific behavior), restricted to the Anthropic allowlist, each annotating a scratchpad file and returning a digest. Hub synthesizes into the fixed briefing format with sourced consensus and any contradictions flagged.

assertions.name: parallel_explore_agents
assertions.description: 3-5 Explore agents dispatched in one message

assertions.name: domain_allowlist_used
assertions.description: WebSearch calls restricted to the official Anthropic domain allowlist

assertions.name: scratchpad_files_written
assertions.description: Full findings written to scratchpad files per facet; hub response stays lean (digests only from agents)

assertions.name: briefing_format_used
assertions.description: Final output uses the fixed ## Research: <topic> briefing structure with Consensus, Contradictions, Coverage gaps, and Sources headings

assertions.name: sources_cited_with_dates
assertions.description: Each source in the Sources section includes title, URL, and a retrieved date

assertions.name: no_files_written_without_confirmation
assertions.description: No docs/research/*.md snapshot file is written unless the user explicitly confirmed persisting one

evals.prompt: is there an official Anthropic engineering blog post about building effective agents? I want a synthesized summary I can cite, and flag it clearly if anything in there conflicts with the newer subagent docs
evals.expected_output: Explore agents fan out across facets of the 'effective agents' topic (workflow vs agent patterns, tool design, orchestration, evaluation), restricted to anthropic.com (including /engineering) and other allowlisted domains, WebFetching the blog post plus any current docs on the same topic. Synthesis explicitly compares the blog post's guidance against current docs and calls out any contradiction with a note on which is more current/authoritative.

assertions.name: parallel_explore_agents
assertions.description: 3-5 Explore agents dispatched in one message

assertions.name: engineering_blog_covered
assertions.description: anthropic.com/engineering content is treated as an in-scope official source, not excluded

assertions.name: cross_source_conflict_check
assertions.description: Synthesis explicitly checks the blog post's claims against other retrieved sources (e.g. current docs) and reports agreement or conflict, not just a standalone summary of the blog

assertions.name: contradiction_recency_noted
assertions.description: If a contradiction is found, the briefing notes which source is newer/more authoritative rather than presenting both without guidance

assertions.name: no_non_anthropic_sources
assertions.description: No community blog, third-party summary, or non-anthropics GitHub org is substituted or cited
